interactions.summary: Sneako proposes future conversations with David Icke, Professor Marandi, and George Galloway, then returns to left/right economics and Pentagon spending. Jiang praises Marandi and says Trump is annoying to liberals because he honestly says domestic care is unaffordable while war continues.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Sneako asks or frames the issue; Jiang answers in the main analytic voice where present.

speaker_notes.note: Machine diarization is consistent in this packet: SPEAKER_00 is Sneako/interviewer and SPEAKER_01 is Jiang, except where intro/outro material is host-only.

claims.claim: Jiang says he is open to long-form good-faith discussion and has respect for Professor Marandi as a passionate and articulate advocate for Iran.
Sneako frames debt, housing, and elite control as right-versus-wrong rather than left-versus-right, contrasting domestic needs with Pentagon spending.
Jiang says Trump’s blunt honesty annoys liberals because he openly admits daycare or Medicare cannot be afforded while America is starting or sustaining the Iran war.

signature_moments.moment: Trump’s truth is ugly because he says the tradeoff in public: no daycare because war comes first.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we can't afford daycare because we're starting... this war in Iran
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It distills empire into a domestic-cost statement.
